rationale: No direct conflict. Section 29.3521 governs appropriations and expenditure caps for the ESA program fund. The homestead exemption increase affects local property tax revenue, not the statutory appropriation mechanism, the $1 billion biennium cap, or the 20% allocation limit in this section.
quote: (c-1) Notwithstanding any other law, for the state fiscal biennium beginning September 1, 2025, the amount spent for purposes of the program may not exceed $1 billion.
_note: No direct statutory conflict. Indirect fiscal pressure possible: increased homestead exemption reduces local ISD property tax revenue, potentially increasing state FSP obligation and competing with the $1B program cap, but no provision of §29.3521 is directly contradicted or rendered unworkable.
